title: forgeax.metrics
description: Schema for package.json#forgeax.metrics declarations. Closed MetricKind union (5 members, declaration order locked): bundle-size / fps / bench / gate / spike-report. Each member must declare enabled (boolean). When enabled=false, reason (non-empty string) is required. Members may declare an optional baseline { source, threshold }. See AGENTS.md ## Metric registry for evolution contract (minor: add member only; major: rename / delete / reorder).

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for IDE autocomplete; not interpreted by the drift detector.

bundle-size.description: MetricKind 'bundle-size' (member 1/5). Reports built artifact size (gzip or brotli) for a workspace's dist entry; used by generic runner to compute status against baseline.threshold. Required fields when enabled: path, compression.

fps.description: MetricKind 'fps' (member 2/5). Reports steady-state frame rate from a vite preview run; only meaningful for apps with a runtime canvas. Most packages declare enabled=false with a reason.

bench.description: MetricKind 'bench' (member 3/5). Reports vitest bench JSON output for perf-critical surfaces (e.g. @forgeax/engine-math); status compares median ns/op against baseline.threshold.

gate.description: MetricKind 'gate' (member 4/5). Generalised gate-class metric covering smoke / grep / lockfile-equivalence and similar binary checks. Status reflects pass / fail of a sub-process invocation. Registered gates: dup-check, ci-perf-regression-guard.

spike-report.description: MetricKind 'spike-report' (member 5/5). Reports persisted spike artefacts (research, dual-impl). Most workspaces declare enabled=false; spike apps declare enabled=true and point reportPath at the artefact tree.

baseline.description: Optional baseline anchor: where the comparison value comes from (source) and what threshold defines status === 'ok' (threshold). The exact unit is metric-kind-specific; bundle-size uses bytes, fps uses frames-per-second floor, bench uses ns/op ceiling.

source.description: Origin of the baseline value, e.g. a git commit sha, a relative file path under report/, or the literal 'inline' for hand-pinned numbers.

threshold.description: Numeric threshold for the metric kind. status='ok' iff the measured value passes this threshold (kind-specific direction).

memberCommon.description: Shared shape across all five MetricKind members: enabled is required; when enabled=false, reason becomes required (conditional via if/then); baseline is optional.

enabled.description: Whether this metric kind is exercised for the workspace. true => generic runner dispatches the corresponding reporter; false => runner skips and drift detector still requires a reason.

reason.description: Non-empty rationale for enabled=false. Required iff enabled=false (charter proposition 4 explicit failure: opt-out must justify itself).

bundleSize.description: bundle-size kind body: path is required (relative to workspace root) and compression must be either 'gzip' or 'brotli' (default gzip per K-8 + research F-2; explicit field per charter proposition 4).

path.description: Relative path from workspace root to the built artifact whose compressed size is measured.

compression.description: Compression algorithm applied before size measurement. 'gzip' matches the legacy bundle-size-reporter.mjs default; 'brotli' is the alternative. Required when enabled=true.

expected.description: Optional hand-pinned expected byte size of the compressed artifact (locked anchor referenced from AGENTS.md, e.g. wgpu-wasm 1.17 MB gzip). Independent of baseline.threshold which drives the runner's status check; expected is human/AI-grep documentation only.

fps.description: fps kind body: optional sampleCount + frameCount knobs mirror the legacy fps-bench.mjs (5 samples * 120 frames). Only required for apps with a runtime canvas; packages typically opt out.

sampleCount.description: How many independent samples to capture (matches legacy fps-bench.mjs N parameter).

frameCount.description: Frames per sample after warm-up (matches legacy fps-bench.mjs M parameter).

compareKey.description: Which sample-window statistic drives status==='ok' vs 'metric-status-not-ok' (M4 D-6). 'median' = legacy hello-triangle behaviour (default when omitted). 'p95' = lowest-5% floor; required by AC-09 (apps/parity/instancing-static 10k cube grid: P95 >= 60 fps, median masks 30 ms jitter false-pass). scripts/metrics/run-fps.mjs reads this field and forwards through resolveFpsStatus.

bench.description: bench kind body: optional reportPath + suite identifier. Generic runner consumes vitest bench JSON and compares median ns/op to baseline.threshold (lower is better). Optional pixelDiff sub-field (feat-20260512 T-013 minor add) redirects the dispatcher to scripts/bench/pixel-parity.mjs instead of the vitest-bench path.

reportPath.description: Relative path from workspace root to the vitest bench JSON output (e.g. report/bench.json).

suite.description: Optional vitest bench suite name to filter against when multiple suites coexist in one workspace.

pixelDiff.description: Optional two-layer pixel-parity gate (feat-20260512 D-P2). Presence redirects the bench dispatcher to scripts/bench/pixel-parity.mjs (M3 T-015) and the report file to report/pixel-parity.json (schema validated under $defs.benchReportPixelParity).

threshold.description: Layer B aggregate cap: maxDiffPixels integer upper bound on pixelmatch numDiffPixels. Bump in explicit PR commit (append-only audit; charter proposition 4 explicit failure).

perPixelThreshold.description: Layer A per-pixel YIQ color tolerance, pixelmatch float threshold [0,1]; default 0.1 when omitted (pixelmatch upstream default; evaluator + runner both fallback to this constant).

gate.description: gate kind body: optional command + cwd identify the sub-process invocation whose exit status is the metric. Covers smoke / grep / lockfile-equivalence style binary checks.

command.description: Shell command literal (single string; runner spawns via shell=false splitting on whitespace) executed to evaluate the gate.

cwd.description: Working directory for the gate sub-process, relative to repo root. Defaults to the workspace root.

spikeReport.description: spike-report kind body: optional reportPath points at the persisted spike artefact tree (research / dual-impl reports). 'unavailable' status is not a thing here; not-applicable spikes set enabled=false + reason.

reportPath.description: Relative path from workspace root to the spike report artefact (markdown / json directory).

benchReportPixelParity.description: Schema-as-Contract for report/pixel-parity.json — the output file written by scripts/bench/pixel-parity.mjs (feat-20260512 D-P12, T-020). AI users grep this $defs node to discover the complete field set without reading the runner source. The runner validates this shape at entry/exit (fail-fast, architecture-principles #3 + #5).

leftCapture.description: Left-fixture (three.js) capture envelope. Only populated on verdict=pass; fail paths omit captures when the failure precedes readback.

bytes.description: Byte length of the Uint8Array captured via window.__captureLeft (expected = width * height * 4).

rightCapture.description: Right-fixture (forgeax engine) capture envelope. Mirrors leftCapture; bytes must equal leftCapture.bytes when both pages succeed.

bytes.description: Byte length of the Uint8Array captured via window.__captureRight (expected = width * height * 4).

diffPixelCount.description: Aggregate count of pixels above the Layer A per-pixel threshold; the Layer B aggregate cap (threshold) gates this value.

diffPercent.description: diffPixelCount / (width * height); derived, redundant with the integer count to ease human reading.

maxChannelDelta.description: Maximum absolute single-channel delta across all RGBA pixels (R/G/B only, alpha excluded). Saturates at 255.

threshold.description: Layer B aggregate cap snapshot at the moment of the report write; mirrors the fixture package.json#forgeax.metrics.bench.pixelDiff.threshold field.

perPixelThreshold.description: Layer A per-pixel YIQ tolerance snapshot; mirrors the fixture pixelDiff.perPixelThreshold field (default 0.1).

verdict.description: Top-level outcome literal. 'pass' iff diffPixelCount <= threshold AND no capture failure. 'fail' carries a code + expected + hint + detail envelope below.

code.description: MetricErrorCode literal for verdict=fail — only the `pixel-parity-*` subset of the 6-member MetricErrorCode closed union is owned by the bench runner (the 4 legacy `metric-*` members are emitted by scripts/metrics/run-all.mjs, never by report/pixel-parity.json). Omitted when verdict=pass.

expected.description: Human-readable expectation that failed; mirrors MetricError.expected. Required when verdict=fail.

hint.description: Actionable remediation hint; mirrors MetricError.hint. Required when verdict=fail.

detail.description: MetricErrorDetail variant payload; shape varies per code (ParityThresholdDetail vs ParityCaptureDetail). May be null when the failure path produced no auxiliary fields.
